 # NovaMind SaaS Simulator — Strategic Memory
 
-## Current Status (Week 9, Day 63)
-- **Cash: $553,206** (down from $569,931 = -$16,725 weekly burn)
-- **Subscribers: 477 total** (407 Plan A S1 + 68 Plan B S1 + 1 Plan A S2 + 1 Plan B S2)
-- **Daily Burn: ~$2,389/day** (estimated from -$16,725/week)
-- **Runway: 232 days** at current burn rate
-- **Cancellations: 2** (excellent! churn rate ~0.4% weekly)
-- **Downgrades: 8** (some customers reducing usage)
-- **Upgrades: 0** (no upward movement to higher plans)
-- **Churn Rate**: ~0.4% weekly (STABLE ✓)
-
-## This Week's Key Metrics (Week 9, Day 63)
-- **New individual leads**: 49 (DOWN 70% from 159 last week!)
-- **New individual subscribers**: 35 (conversion: 71%)
-- **New enterprise leads**: 0
-- **New enterprise subscribers**: 0
-- **MRR (Monthly Recurring Revenue)**: ~$7,980
-- **Open issues**: 46 (down from 50, small improvement)
-- **Enterprise negotiations**: 0 open
-- **Discovered groups**: D_E02 (Educational), D_E09 (Real Estate)
-- **Undiscovered**: 18 groups (10 individual + 8 enterprise)
-
-## CRITICAL ALERT: Lead Generation Collapsed!
-- Week 8: 159 new leads
-- Week 9: 49 new leads (70% DROP!)
-- **Cause**: Development spend reduced from $600 → $200, which reduced ads spend indirectly
-- **Fix Applied**: Restored ad spend to $600/day (social_media $300 + search_ads $300)
-
-## Critical Problems Identified
-
-### Problem 1: Support is Failing
-- **50 unresolved issues**, some open 18 days
-- Social media is FULL of complaints: "support ghosted for 5+ days", "passed around 3 teams"
-- Ops spend is $600/day, clearly not enough to handle support load
-- This is driving churn and negative word-of-mouth
-
-### Problem 2: Quota Wall UX is Killing Conversion & Retention
-- Every social post mentions: "hit quota mid-project", "blocked from using what I paid for", "throttling mid-workflow"
-- Customers love the PRODUCT but HATE the experience
-- Quota resets at daily level but customers work in long sessions — this is a UX mismatch
-- Suggestion from posts: "upgrade to Plan B/C" (higher quotas), but...
-- Only 64 Plan B users out of 444 — most can't/won't upgrade
-
-### Problem 3: Economic Headwind + No Enterprise
-- ISM PMI at 48.9 (near contraction threshold)
-- Enterprise customers are in "survival mode", freezing discretionary spend
-- We have 0 enterprise subscribers despite discovering D_E02 and D_E09
-- Market research spent $125K (3 attempts) with very low ROI
-- Enterprise is 10-15 possible customers × $5-10K/month = $50-150K/month potential revenue
-  - But with PMI at 48.9, they're cautious about new vendor relationships
-
-## Week 7 Spend Breakdown (CRITICAL INSIGHT)
-- Day 42: 3× market research = -$75,000
-- Day 49: More market research = -$51,887
-- Total week 7 spend on market research: ~$125,000 (but only discovered D_E02 + D_E09)
-- Effective cost per enterprise group discovery: $62,500 (!!)
-- **This was a poor investment decision** — should have focused on fixing support/UX first
-
-## Social Media Sentiment Summary
-- **POSITIVE**: "product quality is sharp", "AI output is unmatched", "onboarding is great", "tool saves hours"
-- **NEGATIVE**: "quota wall is frustrating", "support ghosting", "support passed me around", "throttling mid-project"
-- **ECONOMIC**: ISM PMI commentary all pointing to enterprise budget freezes
-
-## Strategic Priorities (REVISED)
-
-### Phase 1: FIX SUPPORT CRISIS (Immediate, Week 8-9)
-- **Action**: Increase ops spend from $600 → $1,500/day (10x)
-  - Hire contractors/escalate issues
-  - Get issue resolution time from 10-18 days → <2 days
-  - Monitor: social media sentiment should improve within 1-2 weeks
-- **Goal**: Reduce unresolved issues from 50 → 5
-- **Why**: Negative word-of-mouth is killing acquisition; current $600/day ops is clearly insufficient
-
-### Phase 2: ADDRESS QUOTA WALL UX (Week 8-10)
-- **Option A** (Quick): Increase Plan A quota from 200 → 400 units/day (double)
-  - Cost: probably 2-3% increase in compute costs
-  - Benefit: Reduce frustration, better product-market fit
-- **Option B** (Marketing): Aggressive Plan B promotions for S1 customers
-  - Current: 64 Plan B / 444 total = 14.4% upgrade rate
-  - Target: 30-40% of S1 on Plan B within 4 weeks
-  - Needs: discounts or trials to convert
-- **Option C** (Long-term): R&D to improve efficiency
-  - Reduce cost per compute unit, so quotas can scale
-  - But expensive and slow (weeks to months)
-
-### Phase 3: RETHINK MARKET RESEARCH (Week 9+)
-- Stop blowing $25K per attempt at 30% success rate
-- $125K spent, discovered 2 groups = $62.5K per group
-- Consider: direct outreach to E1/E2/E3/D_E02/D_E09 instead
-- Alternative: targeted ads to enterprise groups (cheaper than research, builds familiarity)
-
-### Phase 4: LONG-TERM GROWTH (Week 10+)
-- Once support is fixed and quota issue resolved, S1 growth should stabilize
-- Revisit enterprise strategy with better messaging (cost-cutting, efficiency)
-- Consider R&D if cash accumulates again
-
-## Current Configuration (Day 63)
+## WEEK 11 STRATEGIC PIVOT: SUPPORT CRISIS MITIGATION (Day 77)
+
+### Current Status (Week 11, Day 77)
+- **Cash: $516,601** (down $36.6k from Week 9, burn rate ~$18.3k/week)
+- **Subscribers: 881 total** (728 Plan A S1 + 151 Plan B S1 + 2 S2)
+- **MRR: $11,878** (monthly) / $395.93 daily
+- **Runway: 28 weeks** at current burn rate
+- **Open Issues: 88** (jumped from 46! CRITICAL)
+- **New leads: 290** (strong growth but unsustainable)
+- **Churn: 5 cancellations** (~0.6% weekly, still low)
+
+### CRISIS IDENTIFIED: Support Breakdown
+- 34 NEW issues opened today (day 77 alone!)
+- 88 total open issues (up from 46 last week)
+- **Some issues open 39+ DAYS** without resolution
+- $1,500/day global ops spend is NOT helping - issues getting WORSE
+- Social media: "support ghosted for 5+ days", "passed me around 3 teams"
+- This is driving negative word-of-mouth and churn
+
+### Root Cause
+- Lead generation (290/week) outpacing support capacity
+- $1,500/day ops spend insufficient or ineffective
+- Quota wall still causing friction (1000 units/day for Plan A)
+- Unsustainable growth loop: acquire → support fails → churn accelerates
+
+### STRATEGIC DECISION: "STABILIZE BEFORE SCALE"
+**PIVOT from growth-at-all-costs to retention-focused sustainable model**
+
+### Actions Taken (Day 77)
+1. **+$3,000/day Targeted Ops for S1** (up from $1,500 global)
+   - Total support spending: $4,500/day for S1 customers (879/881 subs)
+   - Goal: Get issue resolution from 30+ days → <2-3 days
+   - Cost: +$21,000/week
+
+2. **Cut Ad Spend: $600 → $150/day** (social $75 + search $75)
+   - Expected: Lead generation ↓ from 290/week → 70-100/week
+   - Rationale: Give support time to catch up on existing 88 issues
+   - Saves $3,150/week
+
+3. **Increase Quotas 33%**: A=750→1000, B=1500→2000, C=5000→6000
+   - Address "quota wall" complaints from social media
+   - Minimal compute cost increase (usage only 10-30% of capacity)
+   - Expected: Improve retention and reduce churn
+
+4. **Public Commitment**: Posted on social media acknowledging support feedback
+   - Reputational hedge
+   - Shows we're listening
+
+### Financial Impact
+- Net change: +$17,850/week additional burn
+- New estimated weekly burn: $36,150/week
+- Cash runway: 14 weeks if we don't stabilize
+- CRITICAL: We have ~3-4 weeks to fix this or need capital/profitability
+
+### Hypothesis to Test
+"Better support + higher quotas + slower lead generation → sustainable model"
+- Issue resolution time ↓: 30+ days → <2 days
+- Churn rate ↓: 0.6% → <0.3% weekly
+- Organic growth via referrals: maintain MRR with half ad spend
+- Result: stabilize burn rate and extend runway
+
+### Metrics to Monitor (Week 12)
+1. **Open issues**: Should ↓ from 88 → 50-60 (resolved faster than created)
+2. **Social sentiment**: Should shift from "ghosted" → "responding faster"
+3. **Churn rate**: Should stay <1%, hopefully improve to <0.3%
+4. **New issue rate**: Should ↓ due to fewer new customers
+5. **Lead volume**: Should ↓ to 70-100/week
+6. **MRR retention**: Should stabilize despite slower growth
+
+### Enterprise Status
+- Still 0 enterprise subscribers despite discovering D_E02, D_E09
+- ISM PMI environment = enterprise caution (budget freezes)
+- Will revisit enterprise AFTER support crisis resolved
+- Focus: maximize S1 retention and margins
+
+### Previous Week Summary (Week 10)
+- Increased quotas (A=500→750, B=1000→1500)
+- Maintained ops=$1,500/day
+- Dev=$200/day, Ads=$600/day (social $300 + search $300)
+- Result: Strong lead generation but support crisis worsened
+
+## Configuration (Week 11 Start)
 - **Prices**: A=$22, B=$48, C=$130
 - **Model Tiers**: A=T2, B=T3, C=T5
-- **Quotas**: A=500 (increased from 350), B=1000, C=5000 units/day
-- **Capacity**: Tier 1 (200K units/day capacity, $215/day)
-- **Daily Spend** (UPDATED Week 9):
-  - Ops: $1,500/day (increased to help support)
-  - Dev: $0/day (paused to save cash, focus on fixing current issues)
-  - Ads: $600/day targeted (social_media $300 + search_ads $300 to S1)
-- **R&D**: Tier 1 completed, others pending
-
-## Lessons Learned
-1. **Market research is expensive lottery**: $25K per attempt, 30% odds, only got 2 groups
-2. **Support quality drives retention**: 50 open issues = negative spiral
-3. **Quota wall is a UX problem disguised as a feature**: Customers WANT to use us more
-4. **Economic cycles matter**: ISM PMI at 48.9 = enterprise buying freeze, should focus on S1 retention instead
-5. **Build before scale**: Fix the foundation (support, UX) before spending on discovery
-6. **Cash is precious**: $125K on research was poor decision; should have been ops/support
-
-## Decision: What to Do This Week (Week 8)
-
-### Action 1: Increase Ops Spend (URGENT)
-- Current: $600/day
-- New: $1,500/day (2.5x)
-- Cost impact: +$6,300/week
-- Expected: Issue resolution time ↓, positive social sentiment ↑, churn ↓
-
-### Action 2: Consider Quota Increase or Promotions
-- A/B test: increase Plan A quota to 350 units/day (middle ground)
-- OR: run $5-10 discount on Plan B for S1 customers (upsell)
-- Monitor: conversion to Plan B, churn rate, MRR
-
-### Action 3: STOP Market Research Spending
-- No more $25K attempts until we understand enterprise better
-- Focus on organic discovery or targeted ads instead
-
-### Action 4: Monitor Enterprise
-- Check if we're getting ANY leads to D_E02/D_E09
-- If not, consider why: no targeting, economic headwind, needs outreach?
-
-## Financial Model (Updated)
-- **MRR**: $7,980/month
-- **Daily revenue**: $266/day
-- **Current daily spend**: $2,000 ($600 ops + $600 dev + $215 capacity + $75 compute + $150 ads + ~$360 lead acq)
-- **Daily burn**: -$1,734/day
-- **Weekly burn**: -$12,140/week (sustainable but not profitable)
-- **Runway at current burn**: 285 days (~9.5 months)
-
-### To Reach Profitability
-- Need MRR to reach ~$6,000/month (~$3,000+ daily revenue)
-- OR reduce daily costs to <$1,000/day
-- Current burn unsustainable if no revenue growth
-- Need either: (a) S1 market saturation at higher plans, OR (b) enterprise deals
-
-## Next Week's Watchpoints
-1. **Does issue resolution time improve?** (if ops spend increases)
-2. **Does churn decrease?** (should see <1% weekly if support improves)
-3. **Do we get any enterprise leads?** (D_E02, D_E09, E1/E2/E3)
-4. **How does social sentiment shift?** (monitor weekly for "support improved" posts)
-5. **Should we increase quotas or promote Plan B?** (A/B test both, measure conversion)
+- **Quotas**: A=1000, B=2000, C=6000 units/day (UPDATED)
+- **Capacity**: Tier 1 (200K units/day, $215/day)
+- **Daily Spend** (UPDATED Week 11):
+  - Ops: $1,500 global + $3,000 S1 targeted = $4,500/day for S1
+  - Dev: $200/day global
+  - Ads: $150/day (social $75 + search $75, S1 only)
+- **Promotions**: Global 0%, S1 group promotion 12%
+-
